---
name: runtime-google-fonts
description: PDF and preview fonts load from runtime catalog mapping family -> gstatic TTF URLs; no font files stored in the repo.
keywords: [fonts, pdf, react-pdf, preview, runtime-fonts, gstatic, ttf, google-fonts, scalability]
created: 2026-05-22
updated: 2026-05-24
---

**Fact / Rule:** PDF rendering and browser invoice preview load Google fonts from runtime gstatic TTF URLs declared in `src/lib/pdf/google-fonts-catalog.json` (generated by `pnpm sync-fonts`). No `*.ttf` files are stored in `public/` or anywhere else in the repo. The catalog contains ~720 fonts that support `latin-ext` subset and both 400 + 700 weights.

**Why:** Earlier version of this rule required local TTF files because runtime CSS scraping was unstable: browser fetches of `fonts.googleapis.com/css2` return woff2 (per modern User-Agent), and `@react-pdf/renderer` needs TTF. That concern is resolved by **build-time catalog generation**: `scripts/sync-google-fonts.ts` runs Node-side with empty User-Agent (which forces TTF response) and writes the resulting URLs into the catalog JSON. Runtime browser code never has to scrape CSS — it just looks up TTF URLs from the bundled catalog.

Old (deleted) approach maintained 10 hardcoded font families across three synced locations (`POPULAR_GOOGLE_FONTS` array, `public/fonts/*.ttf` binaries, `src/index.css` `@font-face` blocks). New approach: users can pick any of 720 latin-ext-capable Google fonts; adding more requires only re-running `pnpm sync-fonts`.

**How to apply:**

- Source of truth for available fonts: `src/lib/pdf/google-fonts-catalog.json` (Zod-validated by `src/lib/pdf/googleFonts.ts`).
- Catalog is regenerated by `pnpm sync-fonts` — Node script that fetches `fonts.google.com/metadata/fonts` and per-family CSS endpoint with empty UA. Re-run when new families are needed.
- PDF rendering: `await ensureGoogleFontRegistered(family)` before `pdf(<Doc/>).toBlob()` (see `src/lib/pdf/generateInvoicePdf.tsx`).
- Browser invoice preview: `useGoogleFontInBrowser(family)` hook injects a `<link>` to `fonts.googleapis.com/css2` so the preview canvas matches the PDF.
- Base app UI font (Inter) is loaded once via static `<link>` in `index.html` (preconnect + stylesheet).
- Adding a new font family for users to select requires only a single action: `pnpm sync-fonts`. No code edits, no asset commits.
- Filtering rule: only families with `latin-ext` subset are exposed via `availableFontFamilies` — this guarantees LT character support.
- Validated on 2026-05-24 visually: DM Sans, Inter, Manrope all rendered LT chars (`ąčęėįšųūž ĄČĘĖĮŠŲŪŽ`) correctly in both Regular and Bold weights in `@react-pdf/renderer` PDF output.
- Related: [[lazy-load-pdf-xlsx]] — `@react-pdf/renderer` is dynamically imported by `googleFonts.ts` to preserve code-split.
